name: Population
description: This layer shows total population count by sex and age group. This is shown by tract, county, and state boundaries. This service is updated annually to contain the most currently released American Community Survey (ACS) 5-year data, and contains estimates and margins of error. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis.  <div><br /></div><div>This layer is symbolized to show the percentage of the population that are considered dependent (ages 65+ and &lt;18). To see the full list of attributes available in this service, go to the &quot;Data&quot; tab, and choose &quot;Fields&quot; at the top right. <div><br /></div><div><div><b>Current Vintage</b>: 2019-2023</div><div><b>ACS Table(s)</b>: B01001</div><div><b>Data downloaded from</b>: <a>Census Bureau's API for American Community Survey</a> </div><div><b><br /></b></div><div><b>The United States Census Bureau's American Community Survey (ACS):</b></div><div><ul><li><a>About the Survey</a><br /></li><li><a>Geography &amp; ACS</a><br /></li><li><a>Technical Documentation</a><br /></li><li><a>News &amp; Updates</a><br /></li></ul><div>This ready-to-use layer can be used within ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, its configurable apps, dashboards, Story Maps, custom apps, and mobile apps. Data can also be exported for offline workflows. For more information about ACS layers, visit the <a>FAQ</a>. Please cite the Census and ACS when using this data.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><div><b>Data Note from the Census:</b><br /></div><div>Data are based on a sample and are subject to sampling variability. The degree of uncertainty for an estimate arising from sampling variability is represented through the use of a margin of error. The value shown here is the 90 percent margin of error. The margin of error can be interpreted as providing a 90 percent probability that the interval defined by the estimate minus the margin of error and the estimate plus the margin of error (the lower and upper confidence bounds) contains the true value. In addition to sampling variability, the ACS estimates are subject to nonsampling error (for a discussion of nonsampling variability, see <a>Accuracy of the Data</a>).  The effect of nonsampling error is not represented in these tables.</div></div><div><br /></div><div><b>Data Processing Notes:</b></div><div><ul><li>This layer is updated automatically when the most current vintage of ACS data is released each year, usually in December. The layer always contains the latest available ACS 5-year estimates. It is updated annually within days of the Census Bureau's release schedule. <a>Click here</a> to learn more about ACS data releases.<br /></li><li>Boundaries come from the <a>US Census TIGER geodatabases,</a> specifically, the National Sub-State Geography Database (named tlgdb_(year)_a_us_substategeo.gdb). Boundaries are updated at the same time as the data updates (annually), and the boundary vintage appropriately matches the data vintage <a>as specified by the Census</a>. These are Census boundaries with water and/or coastlines erased for cartographic and mapping purposes. For census tracts, the water cutouts are derived from a subset of the <a>2020 Areal Hydrography boundaries offered by TIGER</a>. Water bodies and rivers which are 50 million square meters or larger (mid to large sized water bodies) are erased from the tract level boundaries, as well as additional important features. For state and county boundaries, the water and coastlines are derived from the coastlines of the 2022 500k <a>TIGER Cartographic Boundary Shapefiles</a>. These are erased to more accurately portray the coastlines and Great Lakes. The original AWATER and ALAND fields are still available as attributes within the data table (units are square meters).</li><li>The States layer contains 52 records - all US states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico</li><li>Census tracts with no population that occur in areas of water, such as oceans, are removed from this data service (Census Tracts beginning with 99).</li><li>Percentages and derived counts, and associated margins of error, are calculated values (that can be identified by the &quot;_calc_&quot; stub in the field name), and abide by the specifications <a>defined by the American Community Survey</a>.</li><li>Field alias names were created based on the Table Shells file available from the <a>American Community Survey Summary File Documentation page</a>.<br /></li><li>Negative values (e.g., -4444...) have been set to null, with the exception of -5555... which has been set to zero. These negative values exist in the raw API data to indicate the following situations:</li><ul><li>The margin of error column indicates that either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute a standard error and thus the margin of error. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>Either no sample observations or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest interval or upper interval of an open-ended distribution.<br /></li><li>The median falls in the lowest interval of an open-ended distribution, or in the upper interval of an open-ended distribution. A statistical test is not appropriate.</li><li>The estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.<br /></li><li>The data for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small.</li></ul></ul><div><br /></div></div></div></div><div><div><div><br /></div><div><div><div><div><div><br /></div><div><br /></div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

fields.name: OBJECTID
fields.description: {"value":"An ObjectID is a unique, not null integer field used to uniquely identify rows in tables in a geographical table.", "fieldValueType":"uniqueIdentifier"}

fields.name: GEOID
fields.description: {"value":"Geographic Identifier, or GEOID is the numeric code used to uniquely identify all administrative/legal and statistical geographic areas for which the Census Bureau tabulates data.","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: ALAND
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of land in square meters within this geographical area\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: AWATER
fields.description: {"value":"Current area of water in square meters within this geographical area","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: NAME
fields.description: {"value":"Name of this geographical area","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: State
fields.description: {"value":"State in which this geography resides","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: County
fields.description: {"value":"County in which this geography resides","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_001E
fields.description: {"value":"Total population includes those living in households, on active duty in the Armed Forces, and those living in group quarters such as correctional facilities, skilled nursing facilities, juvenile facilities, college dorms, etc.","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_001M
fields.description: {"value":"The margins of error are zero because this estimate is controlled. A statistical test for sampling variability is not appropriate.\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_002E
fields.description: {"value":"Male Population. Individuals were asked to mark either \"male\" or \"female\" to indicate their biological sex.\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_002M
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Male Population (90% confidence). Individuals were asked to mark either \"male\" or \"female\" to indicate their biological sex.","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_sexRatioE
fields.description: {"value":"Ratios above 100 indicate a larger male population, and ratios below 100 indicate a larger female population. Individuals were asked to mark either \"male\" or \"female\" to indicate their biological sex. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B01001_002E / B01001_026E , 2 )\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_sexRatioM
fields.alias: Ratio of Males to Females - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for the ratio of males to females (90% confidence). Ratios above 100 indicate a larger male population, and ratios below 100 indicate a larger female population. Individuals were asked to mark either \"male\" or \"female\" to indicate their biological sex. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B01001_026E)*(sqrt((B01001_002M)^2-1.0*((B01001_002E / B01001_026E)^2)*(B01001_026M)^2))\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numLT18E
fields.description: {"value":"Count of People Less Than 18 Years. Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: B01001_003E + B01001_004E + B01001_005E + B01001_006E + B01001_027E + B01001_028E + B01001_029E + B01001_030E\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numLT18M
fields.alias: Population Less than 18 Years - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Count of People Less Than 18 Years (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: sqrt(B01001_003M^2 + B01001_004M^2 + B01001_005M^2 + B01001_006M^2 + B01001_027M^2 + B01001_028M^2 + B01001_029M^2 + B01001_030M^2)\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctLT18E
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of People Less Than 18 Years. Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B01001_calc_numLT18E / B01001_001E , 1 )\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctLT18M
fields.alias: Percent Population Less than 18 Years - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of People Less Than 18 Years (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B01001_001E)*(sqrt((B01001_calc_numLT18M)^2-1.0*((B01001_calc_numLT18E / B01001_001E)^2)*(B01001_001M)^2))\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numGE65E
fields.description: {"value":"Count of People 65 Years and Over. Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: B01001_020E + B01001_021E + B01001_022E + B01001_023E + B01001_024E + B01001_025E + B01001_044E + B01001_045E + B01001_046E + B01001_047E + B01001_048E + B01001_049E\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numGE65M
fields.alias: Population 65 Years and Over - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Count of People 65 Years and Over (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: sqrt(B01001_020M^2 + B01001_021M^2 + B01001_022M^2 + B01001_023M^2 + B01001_024M^2 + B01001_025M^2 + B01001_044M^2 + B01001_045M^2 + B01001_046M^2 + B01001_047M^2 + B01001_048M^2 + B01001_049M^2)\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctGE65E
fields.description: {"value":"Percent of Population that is 65 Years and Over. Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: round( 100.0 * B01001_calc_numGE65E / B01001_001E , 1 )\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctGE65M
fields.alias: Percent Population 65 Years and Over - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of Population that is 65 Years and Over (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B01001_001E)*(sqrt((B01001_calc_numGE65M)^2-1.0*((B01001_calc_numGE65E / B01001_001E)^2)*(B01001_001M)^2))\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numDependE
fields.alias: Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65+)
fields.description: {"value":"Count of Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65 + ). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: B01001_calc_numLT18E + B01001_calc_numGE65E\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_numDependM
fields.alias: Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65+) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Count of Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65 + ) (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: sqrt(B01001_calc_numLT18M^2 + B01001_calc_numGE65M^2)\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctDependE
fields.alias: Percent of Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65+)

fields.name: B01001_calc_pctDependM
fields.alias: Percent of Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65+) - Margin of Error
fields.description: {"value":"Use this value to add and subtract to get the upper and lower bounds for Percent of Population in Dependent Age Groups (under 18 and 65 + ) (90% confidence). Both age and date of birth are used in combination to determine age. Esri calculated field: (100.0 * B01001_001E)*(sqrt((B01001_calc_numDependM)^2-1.0*((B01001_calc_numDependE / B01001_001E)^2)*(B01001_001M)^2))\n\n","fieldValueType":""}

fields.name: B01001_calc_under5E
